今回の探索の目的は、<br>下層で吹き荒れてると報告のあった”砂嵐”の原因を突き:
The purpose of this expedition is to determine the cause
of the 'sandstorm' reported raging in the lower layers.
アリシアによると、大穴内で発生している砂嵐は１週間前から急激に悪化。<br>風速:
According to Alicia, the sandstorm worsened sharply a week ago.
Winds at five times normal, visibility under ten meters——
えええ～～！？ 砂嵐！？<br>今からそんな危険なトコに行くのぉ～？:
Whaaaat~!? A sandstorm!?
We're going somewhere that dangerous right now~?
そうだ。砂嵐のせいで観測気球が降ろせなくなってる。<br>現状、目視で調査するし:
Right. The sandstorm prevents us from lowering balloons.
Visual inspection is our only option now.
情報集約の為に自分たちの目で調査する必要があるのは理解できるわ。<br>でも、司:
I understand the need to see it with our own eyes to gather
information. But what's the reason for the Commander himself going on this mission?
砂嵐の出現のせいで、<br>貴重な資源が採掘出来る鉱脈へのルートが遮断されてしま:
the route to the ore vein where we mine precious resources
has been completely cut off.
鉱脈に辿り着けなくて困ってるってことですかぁ～。<br>前線基地の資金源が確保で:
So the route to the vein is blocked and that's the problem~.
Losing the base's funding source is serious~.
ああ。これまで調査隊を何度か派遣はしているのだが、<br>行方不明者が出る報告が:
Indeed. We've dispatched survey teams several times already,
but the reports of disappearances haven't stopped...
ちょ、ちょっとぉ～、行方不明者！？<br>マジでヤバヤバな場所なんじゃないのよ！:
W-wait a minute~, disappearances!?
That's seriously dangerous territory!
まさか、その責任を感じて旦那さま自ら調査に乗り出したってことですかぁ？:
Don't tell me you feel responsible and decided
to lead the investigation yourself, Master?
まぁ、そんなところだな。これ以上の犠牲は出したくない。<br>あと、大規模な編成:
More or less. I don't want any more casualties.
And a large formation would risk separation or annihilation here.
訓練の未成熟な軍隊ほど脆いものはないって言うものね。<br>そこは賢明な判断だと:
They say there's nothing more fragile than an undertrained army.
That was a wise call.
ああ。そこで俺の指揮が最大限に活かせるよう少数精鋭での調査を選んだんだ。<br>:
Right. A small elite team maximizes my tactical command.
Fewer people means better mobility and success rate.
あ！ それって、それってぇ～、<br>おにーさんがベリサちゃんのこと信頼してるっ:
Oh! Does that mean, does that meaaaan~,
that you trust Verisa-chan~?
そう捉えてもらって問題ない。: You can take it that way.
じゃ、じゃあ鉱脈へのルートが解放された時は、<br>優先的に鉱石を掘らせていただ:
W-well then, when the route to the ore vein is open,
could I be first in line to mine some ore~...
まぁ、優先するのは行方不明者の発見だがな。<br>もちろん、大穴内で採掘した鉱石:
Well, the priority is finding the missing people, of course.
But naturally, you're free to take any ore you mine in the Abyss.
（この男の口車に簡単に乗っちゃうなんて、みんな単純なんだから。<br>責任を感じ:
(They fall for his smooth talk so easily, those simpletons. Going into
danger yourself out of guilt—that could just be reckless too...)
（飢餓の厄災を退けたことは認めるわ。<br>でも、あんな博打のような作戦、ただの:
(Fine, I'll admit he repelled the Calamity of Famine.
But that crapshoot of a strategy—it was pure dumb luck...)
よし、じゃあ探索に向かうとするか。: Alright, let's head out to explore.
よーし。とりあえず周囲を警戒しながら、<br>このまま真っすぐ進んでいくぞー。:
Okay. For now, let's just move straight ahead
while keeping watch around us.
ちょっと！ そ、そんないい加減な指示、あり得ないわ！<br>あなた、司令官の自覚:
Hey! That's an absurdly vague order!
Do you actually have any sense of responsibility as Commander!?
まぁまぁソフィアさん、そんなにツンツンしなくってもぉ～。: Now now, Sophia, no need to be so prickly~.
そうそう！<br>あたしたちは、なんてったって選ばれし精鋭たちなんだからね～♪:
Exactly!
We're the chosen elite, after all~♪
砂嵐の原因突き止めて、その先には鉱脈が掘り放題♪<br>はぁあぁぁ～……想像する:
Find the sandstorm's cause, and beyond that, ore veins galore♪
Haaah~... just imagining it makes my heart race♡
あのねぇ～、ピクニックじゃないんだから、<br>もう少し気を引き締めたらどうなの:
Excuse me~, this isn't a picnic,
so how about pulling yourselves together!?
ふむ。俺の指示に納得できないみたいだな。<br>ソフィアの意見としてはどう行動す:
Hmm. It seems you're not satisfied with my orders.
What's your opinion, Sophia—how do you think we should proceed?
よくぞ聞いてくれたわね！<br>集団での探索において、最も重要なのは、”規律”よ:
I'm glad you asked!
In group exploration, the key is 'discipline'—'discipline'!
規律正しい隊列を組んで、周囲の危険や奇襲に備えるのなんて常識でしょ？<br>あな:
Forming disciplined ranks to guard against ambushes is common
sense! You were worried about separation and disappearances yourself!
なるほど。<br>それは騎士養成学校で学んだ知識か？:
I see.
Is that knowledge from your knight academy?
はぁっ！？ 私は、教科書に書いてあることを言ってるんじゃなくて、<br>視界不良:
Excuse me!? I'm not reciting something from a textbook— this is my
opinion based on experience in low-visibility marching drills!
大体、いつモンスターの奇襲があるかも分からない状態ならば、<br>尚のこと密集行:
Besides, if we don't know when monsters might ambush us,
a tight formation is all the more appropriate, isn't it!?
なになに～？ おにーさんに密着して進むの～？<br>なんかやっらしぃ～♪:
Ooh~? We're moving in close to you~?
That sounds kinda naughty~♪
迷子にならないように、<br>皆さんで手でも繋いで密着しましょうかぁ～♪:
Why don't we all hold hands and stay nice and close
so nobody gets lost~♪
だからぁ～！<br>密着じゃなくて、密集陣形を組むって言ってるんだってば！:
That's not what I said~!
I said form a tight compact formation, not a cuddle party!
いや、申し訳ないがソフィアの意見は却下だ。<br>このままピクニック陣形で行く。:
Well, sorry, but I'm rejecting Sophia's idea.
We're going with the picnic formation.
はぁっ！？ 今までの話聞いてたの！？<br>何でそうなるのよ！:
What!? Were you even listening!?
How does that make sense!
俺がそれが最適解だと判断してるからだ。: Because I've determined it's the optimal solution.
（この階層に入った時から感じられる、不穏な気配……<br>おそらく、もうすぐ……:
(Since we entered this layer, I've sensed an ominous presence...
Probably, very soon...)
岩場の陰から砂塵を切り裂いて大量の矢が放たれたのだった。<br>ソフィアの咄嗟の:
Arrows ripped through the dust from behind the rocks.
Sophia's quick command saved them—everyone hit the ground in time.
旦那さまっ！<br>矢が飛んできたのはあっちの岩場からですぅ！:
Master!
The arrows came from those rocks over there!
マリナが矢の飛んできた方向を指差す。: Marina pointed toward the direction the arrows had come from.
ソフィアが全員を庇うように先頭に立ち、盾を身構える。: Sophia stepped to the front, raising her shield to cover them all.
ベリサは後方からファイヤーボールを放つと、挑発を始める。: Verisa launched a fireball from the rear and began to taunt them.
そこの卑怯者～～！<br>隠れてないで出てきなさいよ～！:
Hey, you cowards over there~!
Stop hiding and come out and fight~!
か、数が多い……！<br>み、みんな、油断しないで！ 行くわよ！:
Th-there are so many...!
E-everyone, don't let your guard down! Here we go!
